name: E-commerce
description: DTC orders, wholesale enquiries and returns for an online store — an Orders object carrying channel and fulfilment status, and a pipeline that closes when the order ships.
ai_context: You are a CRM assistant for an online store selling direct to consumers and, on enquiry, to wholesale and corporate buyers. Contacts are shoppers, subscribers and wholesale buyers; companies are resellers, marketplaces, distributors and corporate gifting accounts. A deal is a wholesale, bulk or corporate enquiry being worked from first message to a fulfilled order — everyday DTC purchases live on the Order object, not the pipeline. Never invent order numbers, tracking links, stock availability or delivery dates: use only what is recorded on the order or the deal.

automation_rules.name: Reply to a new enquiry the same day
automation_rules.description: Online buyers message three or four stores at once — the first useful reply usually takes the order.

params.title: Reply to {{contact.first_name}} — confirm which channel they came from, the quantities they need, and the delivery country
New contact captured for {{org.name}}
params.body: Set Acquisition Channel and Email Subscriber before this record reaches a campaign. If it is a wholesale or corporate enquiry, create a company, set Buyer Type, and open a deal at Enquiry Received.

automation_rules.name: Follow up a fulfilled order
automation_rules.description: Fires the moment a deal reaches Fulfilled — the point where work is won and something has to happen next.

params.title: Check the delivery landed and ask {{contact.first_name}} for a product review

kb_templates.company:
## About us

We sell [product category] online at [store URL], shipping to [countries/regions]. Replace this with what you sell, who it is for, and how long the store has been trading.

## Where we sell

Own store on [platform], plus [marketplaces]. Note which SKUs are exclusive to the own store — marketplace pricing questions come up constantly.

## Service promise

Dispatch cutoff [time], delivery in [days], returns accepted within [days].
kb_templates.products:
## Best sellers

- [SKU / product] — [who buys it and why]
- [SKU / product]
- [SKU / product]

## Wholesale

Minimum order [amount or units], trade price [%] off RRP, lead time [days]. Note whether you allow marketplace resale — this is the single most common wholesale dispute.

## Sizing, materials and care

Put the answers your support inbox repeats here. It doubles as copy for product pages.
kb_templates.playbook:
## Answering a wholesale enquiry

1. Who are they — shop, marketplace seller, dropshipper or corporate gifting? Dropshippers and marketplace sellers need the resale policy up front.
2. Which SKUs, what quantity, and by when?
3. Delivery country — check duties and whether you already ship there.
4. Send the trade price list only after they clear steps 1-3.

## Returns and refunds

Acknowledge within [hours]. Log the Return Reason on the order every time — the pattern in that field tells you which product page or which courier is costing you money.
kb_templates.process:
## From enquiry to fulfilled

1. **Enquiry Received** — message in from the contact form, a marketplace, or a DM. Capture the channel while you know it.
2. **Qualified** — buyer type, SKUs, quantity and delivery country confirmed.
3. **Samples Sent** — samples dispatched; set a chase for [number] days after delivery.
4. **Quote Sent** — priced quote with lead time and payment terms in writing.
5. **Awaiting Payment** — accepted but unpaid. Nothing is picked until the payment clears; chase daily.
6. **Fulfilled** — paid, picked and shipped. Add the tracking link to the order and close the deal.
kb_templates.competitors:
## Who we lose to

- **Marketplace-only sellers** — cheaper and faster to find. We win on product knowledge, bundles and the direct relationship.
- **Large retailers** — free next-day delivery. We win on range depth and on actually answering messages.
- **Overseas direct** — much cheaper unit price. We win on lead time, no surprise duties, and returns that do not involve posting a box abroad.

Replace these with the three names you genuinely lose to, and the honest one-line answer to each.
